name: Xpiacoc
description: Xpiacoc, alongside his consort Xmucane, is a creator god in Mayan mythology, particularly prominent in the Popol Vuh. He is considered one of the divine grandparents and is associated with the creation of humans from maize. Xpiacoc is often depicted as an old man, wise and knowledgeable, possessing the power of divination and the ability to predict the future. His role in the creation myth involves grinding the maize and helping to shape the first humans. He is a key figure in understanding the Mayan worldview, representing the wisdom of the ancestors and the importance of maize in their culture. Xpiacoc's influence extends beyond the initial creation, as he continues to be a symbol of knowledge, age, and the cyclical nature of life. He embodies the wisdom passed down through generations and the crucial role of the divine in shaping the mortal world. He is also considered a soothsayer and diviner, able to interpret omens and guide the Mayan people. Xpiacoc and Xmucane together represent the balance of male and female energies in the creation process, highlighting the interconnectedness of all things in the Mayan cosmos. His legacy endures as a reminder of the divine origins of humanity and the enduring power of the ancestors.
subtitle: Creator God and Divine Grandparent

keyMyths.title: Creation of Humans
keyMyths.description: Xpiacoc and Xmucane grind the maize to create the first humans, imbuing them with life and intelligence. This act is central to the Mayan creation story.

keyMyths.title: Divination and Prophecy
keyMyths.description: Xpiacoc is known for his ability to predict the future and interpret omens, guiding the Mayan people with his divine wisdom. He is consulted for guidance in times of uncertainty.

keyMyths.title: The Divine Grandparents
keyMyths.description: Together with Xmucane, Xpiacoc represents the wise ancestors who oversee the world and ensure the continuation of life. They are revered for their knowledge and guidance.
